patch:
diff --git a/sabnzbd/api.py b/sabnzbd/api.py
index 30982b0b3eb..1a5c90f41bf 100644
--- a/sabnzbd/api.py
+++ b/sabnzbd/api.py
@@ -1895,7 +1895,13 @@ def build_history(
 
 def add_active_history(postproc_queue: List[NzbObject], items: List[Dict[str, Any]]):
     """Get the active history queue and add it to the existing items list"""
+    nzo_ids = set([nzo["nzo_id"] for nzo in items])
+
     for nzo in postproc_queue:
+        # Skip already in history
+        if nzo.nzo_id in nzo_ids:
+            continue
+
         # This output has to be the same as fetch_history!
         item = {
             "completed": int(time.time()),
test_patch:
diff --git a/tests/test_api_and_interface.py b/tests/test_api_and_interface.py
index 3e42563708c..586f8afae27 100644
--- a/tests/test_api_and_interface.py
+++ b/tests/test_api_and_interface.py
@@ -259,3 +259,25 @@ def test_add_active_history_consistency(self):
             history_keys = list(history_job.keys())
             history_keys.sort()
             assert pp_keys == history_keys
+
+    @pytest.mark.usefixtures("run_sabnzbd")
+    def test_add_active_history_duplicate(self):
+        """Verify that add_active_history does not add duplicate entries"""
+        history_db = os.path.join(SAB_CACHE_DIR, DEF_ADMIN_DIR, DB_HISTORY_NAME)
+        with FakeHistoryDB(history_db) as fake_history:
+            fake_history.add_fake_history_jobs(1)
+            jobs, total_items = fake_history.fetch_history()
+            history_job = jobs[-1]
+
+            # Add minimal attributes to create pp-job
+            nzo = mock.Mock()
+            nzo.nzo_id = history_job["nzo_id"]
+            nzo.final_name = "test_add_active_history"
+            nzo.repair, nzo.unpack, nzo.delete = pp_to_opts(choice(list(PP_LOOKUP.keys())))
+            nzo.download_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(db.HistoryDB.db_path), "placeholder_downpath")
+            nzo.bytes_downloaded = randint(1024, 1024**4)
+            nzo.unpack_info = {"unpack_info": "placeholder unpack_info line\r\n" * 3}
+            api.add_active_history([nzo], jobs)
+
+            # Make sure the job was not added to the list, a completed entry already exists
+            assert total_items == len(jobs)
problem_statement:
Occasional calls to history API returning excess entries ### SABnzbd version 4.5.2 ### Operating system Unraid/Docker ### Using Docker image linuxserver ### Description Every so often, a call to the SAB history API has been returning two entries for the same NZO_ID, with both of them marked as a status of "Completed" but one of them missing the storage path. Calling the API again shortly after returns only the one entry (the second slot). Apologies if I'm misunderstanding and this is an intentional response, but I assumed that for a Completed download it should have one history entry and know the final destination. I can't consistently reproduce the timing that results in this happening, but am getting it in around 1/100 loops of post processing from mylar when it checks the history queue. ```json { "history":{ "total_size":"14.8 T", "month_size":"412.8 G", "week_size":"40.9 G", "day_size":"14.9 G", "slots":[ { "completed":1752669335, "name":"Angela.-.Asgards.Assassin.004.2015.Digital.Zone-Empire", "nzb_name":"Angela.-.Asgards.Assassin.004.2015.Digital.Zone-Empire.nzb", "category":"comics", "pp":"D", "script":"Default", "report":"", "url":"http://servermonkey3.home.arpa:8090/api?apikey=--REDACTED--&cmd=downloadNZB&nzbname=Angela.-.Asgards.Assassin.004.2015.Digital.Zone-Empire.nzb", "status":"Completed", "nzo_id":"SABnzbd_nzo_h4h4kvnz", "storage":"", "path":"/media/downloads/usenet/incomplete/Angela.-.Asgards.Assassin.004.2015.Digital.Zone-Empire", "script_line":"", "download_time":3, "postproc_time":0, "stage_log":[ { "name":"Source", "actions":[ "http://servermonkey3.home.arpa:8090/api?apikey=--REDACTED--&cmd=downloadNZB&nzbname=Angela.-.Asgards.Assassin.004.2015.Digital.Zone-Empire.nzb" ] }, { "name":"Download", "actions":[ "Downloaded in 3 seconds at an average of 14.2 MB/s
Age: 3680d" ] }, { "name":"Servers", "actions":[ "news.usenetserver.com=46.9 MB" ] }, { "name":"Repair", "actions":[ "[Angela-Asgards.Assassin.004.(2015).(Digital).(Zone-Empire)] Quick Check OK", "Trying RAR renamer" ] } ], "downloaded":49282231, "completeness":"None", "fail_message":"", "url_info":"", "bytes":49282231, "size":"47.0 MB", "meta":"None", "series":"", "duplicate_key":"angela", "md5sum":"", "password":"None", "action_line":"Moving: Angela - Asgard's Assassin 004 (2015) (Digital) (Zone-Empire).cbr", "loaded":false, "retry":false, "archive":false }, { "completed":1752669335, "name":"Angela.-.Asgards.Assassin.004.2015.Digital.Zone-Empire", "nzb_name":"Angela.-.Asgards.Assassin.004.2015.Digital.Zone-Empire.nzb", "category":"comics", "pp":"D", "script":"Default", "report":"", "url":"http://servermonkey3.home.arpa:8090/api?apikey=--REDACTED--&cmd=downloadNZB&nzbname=Angela.-.Asgards.Assassin.004.2015.Digital.Zone-Empire.nzb", "status":"Completed", "nzo_id":"SABnzbd_nzo_h4h4kvnz", "storage":"/media/downloads/usenet/comics/Angela.-.Asgards.Assassin.004.2015.Digital.Zone-Empire/Angela - Asgard's Assassin 004 (2015) (Digital) (Zone-Empire).cbr", "path":"/media/downloads/usenet/incomplete/Angela.-.Asgards.Assassin.004.2015.Digital.Zone-Empire", "script_line":"", "download_time":3, "postproc_time":0, "stage_log":[ { "name":"Source", "actions":[ "http://servermonkey3.home.arpa:8090/api?apikey=--REDACTED--&cmd=downloadNZB&nzbname=Angela.-.Asgards.Assassin.004.2015.Digital.Zone-Empire.nzb" ] }, { "name":"Download", "actions":[ "Downloaded in 3 seconds at an average of 14.2 MB/s
Age: 3680d" ] }, { "name":"Servers", "actions":[ "news.usenetserver.com=46.9 MB" ] }, { "name":"Repair", "actions":[ "[Angela-Asgards.Assassin.004.(2015).(Digital).(Zone-Empire)] Quick Check OK", "Trying RAR renamer" ] } ], "downloaded":49282231, "completeness":"None", "fail_message":"", "url_info":"", "bytes":49282231, "meta":"None", "series":"None", "md5sum":"798feef96d14b4d612286f08abc969f8", "password":"None", "duplicate_key":"angela", "archive":false, "size":"47.0 MB", "action_line":"", "loaded":false, "retry":false } ], "ppslots":1, "noofslots":1, "last_history_update":15933, "version":"4.5.2" } } ```
hints_text:
Can you inspect history1.db if the entry is there once or twice? If twice there (which I expect), IMHO it's not the API, but the logging into history1.db. 

`~/.sabnzbd/admin$ sqlite3 history1.db .dump | grep -i angela`

or use `sqlitebrowser` for GUI / interactive.

EDIT:

> Calling the API again shortly after returns only the one entry (the second slot).

Oh, I missed that. If so, then probably an API problem indeed.


No worries, always worth checking belt and braces.  Only one entry in the DB.

One other thing I noted was the "action_line" entry on the bogus slot, which made me wonder if it was a transactional thing when the status is being updated to completed after the file move, and it's just bad timing to have hit that microsecond as it's upating the status / creating a new history entry for the final completed data.  It would tally with the infrequent nature of the problem.
> One other thing I noted was the "action_line" entry on the bogus slot, which made me wonder if it was a transactional thing when the status is being updated to completed after the file move, and it's just bad timing to have hit that microsecond as it's upating the status / creating a new history entry for the final completed data. It would tally with the infrequent nature of the problem.

Indeed.

And the first entry indeed shows  `"action_line":"Moving: ... `, so work in progress?

```
$ diff 1.json 2.json
12c12
<             "storage":"",
---
>             "storage":"/media/downloads/usenet/comics/Angela.-.Asgards.Assassin.004.2015.Digital.Zone-Empire/Angela - Asgard's Assassin 004 (2015) (Digital) (Zone-Empire).cbr",
49d48
<             "size":"47.0 MB",
51,53c50,51
<             "series":"",
<             "duplicate_key":"angela",
<             "md5sum":"",
---
>             "series":"None",
>             "md5sum":"798feef96d14b4d612286f08abc969f8",
55c53,56
<             "action_line":"Moving: Angela - Asgard's Assassin 004 (2015) (Digital) (Zone-Empire).cbr",
---
>             "duplicate_key":"angela",
>             "archive":false,
>             "size":"47.0 MB",
>             "action_line":"",
57,58c58
<             "retry":false,
<             "archive":false
---
>             "retry":false
```


this user came on discord, turns out they have another issue with .cbr wrongly getting viewed as obfuscated rar (CBR/CBZ are, iirc, literally just rars and zips, they just have different extensions to indicate their contents). 

```
2025-07-16 17:05:50,911::DEBUG::[postproc:965] Detected volume-number 340 from RAR-header: /media/downloads/usenet/incomplete/Howard The Duck 003 (2015) (Digital) (BlackManta-Empire) (cbr)-slug/Howard The Duck 003 (2015) (Digital) (BlackManta-Empire).cbr 
2025-07-16 17:05:50,915::DEBUG::[postproc:980] Deobfuscate: rarvolnr is: {340: {'/media/downloads/usenet/incomplete/Howard The Duck 003 (2015) (Digital) (BlackManta-Empire) (cbr)-slug/Howard The Duck 003 (2015) (Digital) (BlackManta-Empire).cbr': ['Howard The Duck 003 (2015) (Digital) (BlackManta-Empire)/Howard The Duck (2015-) 003-000.jpg', 'Howard The Duck 003 (2015) (Digital) (BlackManta-...
Empire)/z.jpg']}}
...
2025-07-16 17:05:50,916::DEBUG::[postproc:995] Deobfuscate: Just one obfuscated rarset
2025-07-16 17:05:50,916::DEBUG::[postproc:1000] Deobfuscate: Renaming /media/downloads/usenet/incomplete/Howard The Duck 003 (2015) (Digital) (BlackManta-Empire) (cbr)-slug/Howard The Duck 003 (2015) (Digital) (BlackManta-Empire).cbr to /media/downloads/usenet/incomplete/Howard The Duck 003 (2015) (Digital) (BlackManta-Empire) (cbr)-slug/Howard The Duck 003 (2015) (Digital) (BlackManta-Empire) (cbr)-slug.r338
```

which is fallout of the .cbr being checked and viewed as a rar so when it does the rar header detection in rarvolinfo, it returning that its volume number is 340 (which then logic then goes oh it should be -2 so .r338) then that gets used to rename later on. 
https://github.com/sabnzbd/sabnzbd/blob/develop/sabnzbd/utils/rarvolinfo.py

tried having them set cbr as ignore extension (config>special>ext_rename_ignore) but did not change the outcome as its not our actual deobfustication stuff but during the rar renamer 
https://github.com/sabnzbd/sabnzbd/blob/develop/sabnzbd/postproc.py#L963

as .cbr are a little special we might want to guard against these from being checked in the rar_renamer to prevent this. which might end up resolving this other issue as well.. will piece this out to its own issue
I think it's a timing issue indeed. Right between Post processing finishing and the history item being added. 
We don't have locking there due to performance. But this is the fallout.
Alternatively to it being double, it would dissappear while being moved. Also bad..
> No worries, always worth checking belt and braces. Only one entry in the DB.
> 
> One other thing I noted was the "action_line" entry on the bogus slot, which made me wonder if it was a transactional thing when the status is being updated to completed after the file move, and it's just bad timing to have hit that microsecond as it's upating the status / creating a new history entry for the final completed data. It would tally with the infrequent nature of the problem.

other apps have a few checks to make sure its safe to proceed with sab history items. 

only process if see if status is Failed or Completed
For Failed, they check the FailMessage to see why.. then figure out if you want to warn user/remove and retry or whatever. 
For Completed, they make sure Storage path is not empty (since you cant really process a folder that it is not set). 

I'd imagine that this alone would prevent your issue
Funnily enough I added that latter check as a PR this morning - checks for empty storage on Completed jobs, gives it a little wait before rechecking.  It's been running without hitting this problem again all day in my test environment.  Happy to leave it at that if the alternative is making detrimental changes on the SAB side.
From what I can tell, build_history adds the active entries after getting the finished entries so you could skip the already existing nzo_ids in add_active_history().
> From what I can tell, build_history adds the active entries after getting the finished entries so you could skip the already existing nzo_ids in add_active_history().

Good idea. Want to implement it?

Sorry, this seems like something you'll want new tests for and I hate writing tests.
